forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system over the Tasman Sea will maintain a ridge across South Australia through to Friday. Northerly winds freshen on Friday ahead of a trough that is forecast to move across the far west Friday afternoon, then continue across the state during the weekend.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Easterly 10 to 15 knots turning north to northeasterly during the morning and early afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy.
Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots tending northerly in the early morning then tending southeast to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning west to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots during the morning then becoming southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: East to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending north to northeasterly during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy.
North to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Streaky Bay during the morning.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northerly 15 to 25 knots turning westerly 15 to 20 knots during the afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.text: Easterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly in the late morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending northerly 15 to 20 knots before dawn. Winds reaching up to 25 knots offshore during the morning and early afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northerly 20 to 25 knots turning west to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
1 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
Easterly 15 to 20 knots turning north to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly 15 to 20 knots early in the morning. Winds reaching up to 25 knots in the west in the late morning and early afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots tending northwesterly during the afternoon.
1 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
